Annuities, Pensions, and Joint and Survivor Annuity Elections, §§ 404–407, , provided that the rights of persons to whom pensions or annuities were awarded before were to be governed by the applicable provisions of law in effect prior to that date, that the election of a joint and survivor annuity made before , by a person to whom the annuity accrued before , was to be given effect as though the provisions of law under which the election was made had continued to be operative, that death payments under sections 219 and 228e of this title, other than survivor annuities pursuant to an election, were to be made only with respect to deaths occurring before , and that any person to whom an annuity accrued before , and who would as of the date of the initial accrual have been entitled to an annuity in a greater amount by reason of the amendments by act , had such amendments been in effect at the date of initial accrual, was to be awarded the annuity in such greater amount without additional application therefor.Act July 31, 1946, ch. 70960 Stat. 742July 31, 1946July 31, 1946Jan. 1, 1947Jan. 1, 1947Jan. 1, 1947July 31, 1946

Reduction of Annuity Because of Prior Disability Annuity Terminated by Recovery, , provided that no annuities accruing after July 1946 were to be reduced under in order to compensate for an annuity terminated by recovery from a disability.Act July 31, 1946, ch. 709, § 40860 Stat. 742section 228b(a)(3) of this title

Dual Benefit Provision; Retroactive Payment to Survivors, , provided that in the case of a decedent dying before , so much of any annuity or pension payment due such decedent under , was to be paid only to the widow or widower of the decedent, if living, or to the child or children of such decedent, in the alternative, if living.Act June 16, 1954, ch. 300, § 268 Stat. 250section 228c(b) of this titleJuly 1, 1954

Percentage Adjustment, , , authorized the Railroad Retirement Board to make certain adjustments in allowance percentages under this subchapter, so that the percentages when applied against certain social security benefits, would result in amounts comparable to those in effect prior to the changes made by , , , known as the Social Security Amendments of 1971.Pub. L. 92–46, § 485 Stat. 101Pub. L. 92–585 Stat. 5July 2, 1971Mar. 17, 1971

L. 86–28section 228z–1 of this titleMar. 19, 1959, , provided that all recertifications by the Board under act were to be made without applications therefor unless required by reason of section 9 of act , in which case, recertifications were to be made only upon application therefor in such manner and form as the Board was to prescribe.Act Oct. 30, 1951, ch. 632, § 25(j)65 Stat. 691Oct. 30, 1951Oct. 30, 1951, , provided in part that all recertifications required by reason of act were to be made by the Board without application therefor.Act June 23, 1948, ch. 608, § 362 Stat. 577June 23, 1948

Restrictions on Establishment of New Annuities and Use of Certain Labor Tactics, , , placed certain limitations on the utilization of particular procedures established under the Railway Labor Act, section 151 et seq. of this title, when alterations in the provisions of this subchapter regarding certain annuity payments were being sought and placed similar limitations on the use of strikes and lockouts as labor practices when such changes were being sought.Pub. L. 91–215, § 784 Stat. 72Mar. 17, 1970
